context.description: Reflective prose argues that people construct a coherent self-narrative after the fact to make life feel consistent. It compares neuroscience with mythology and biblical symbolism, then turns toward the body as a necessary part of understanding illusion and selfhood.
normalization_notes: Source is German prose from a book page.
Promoted book title from the source file name.

rows.paragraph_text: People construct a coherent story about themselves after the fact, adapting explanations to facts so the narrative appears seamless and free of contradiction.
rows.text_content: People construct a coherent story about themselves after the fact, adapting explanations to facts so the narrative appears seamless and free of contradiction.

rows.paragraph_text: The passage links neuroscience to mythology and the Bible, arguing that both use different languages to describe symbolic structures of human existence.
rows.text_content: The passage links neuroscience to mythology and the Bible, arguing that both use different languages to describe symbolic structures of human existence.

rows.paragraph_text: It then shifts toward the body and insists that any account of illusion and self-understanding must include the material role of the body.
rows.text_content: It then shifts toward the body and insists that any account of illusion and self-understanding must include the material role of the body.
